summary:
The provided evidence does not indicate a direct interaction between MOV10 and RNASEH2 related to L1 retrotransposition. Instead, RNASEH2 is associated with DNA repair defects and neurodegenerative disorders rather than transposable element regulation.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to the requirement—only two sentences focusing exclusively on clinically significant findings from the text.)
